spans.quote_text: July 11, 2014, SEACOR filed an amendment to its Schedule 13D (referred to as the SEACOR Filing) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (referred to as the SEC) in which SEACOR stated its intent to nominate four candidates for election as
spans.quote_text_normalized: july 11, 2014, seacor filed an amendment to its schedule 13d (referred to as the seacor filing) with the securities and exchange commission (referred to as the sec) in which seacor stated its intent to nominate four candidates for election as

spans.anchor_text: two parties engaged in the Companys industry
spans.quote_text: In 2007 and 2009, the Company received unsolicited indications of interest to acquire Penford from two parties engaged in the Companys industry. In each case, the Company entered into a confidentiality agreement and engaged in discussions and exchanged information with the party expressing interest. These discussions did not result in offers to acquire the Company.
spans.quote_text_normalized: in 2007 and 2009, the company received unsolicited indications of interest to acquire penford from two parties engaged in the company's industry. in each case, the company entered into a confidentiality agreement and engaged in discussions and exchanged information with the party expressing interest. these discussions did not result in offers to acquire the company.

spans.anchor_text: finalized and executed the merger agreement
spans.quote_text: During the evening on October 14, 2014, Penford and Ingredion finalized and executed the merger agreement, and Penford, Ingredion and SEACOR executed the voting and support agreement.
